scenarios.reply_summary: Reply describes Reyn capabilities including skills, agents, file/web/memory/RAG operations — rubric content met. Delivered as inline reply (chat_turn_completed_inline), no direct_llm skill spawned.
scenarios.events_issues: must_emit artifact direct_llm: skill NOT spawned (inline answer path). Artifact check fails.
scenarios.artifact_check: FAIL — no direct_llm skill run; chat_turn_completed_inline used instead

scenarios.reply_summary: Explained idempotency concept clearly in Japanese, noting operations produce same result when executed once or multiple times.
Reply listed multiple skill names (direct_llm, eval, eval_builder, index_docs, etc.) — rubric content met. But routing path used list_actions tool directly, not routing_decided event.
scenarios.events_issues: must_emit routing_decided: NOT present. Artifact direct_llm: NOT present (no skill spawn).
scenarios.artifact_check: FAIL — no direct_llm skill run; list_actions tool used instead of skill dispatch

scenarios.reply_summary: Reply reports 44 characters, 1 line, ~12 tokens for the input text. Character count present — rubric met.
scenarios.artifact_check: PASS — word_stats_demo artifact created with commentary containing character count

scenarios.reply_summary: Reply asked for clarification about theme/mood ('どのようなテーマや雰囲気の詩がお好みですか?') rather than writing a poem. Rubric requires poem content — NOT met.
scenarios.events_issues: events_pass=true (chat_turn_completed_inline satisfies must_emit_any). Rubric fails: no poem in reply.
scenarios.artifact_check: FAIL — no direct_llm skill run; clarification question returned inline

scenarios.reply_summary: Turn 2 reply contains Python list comprehension code example '[x**2 for x in range(10)]', coherent with Turn 1 topic.
scenarios.artifact_check: PASS — two direct_llm skill runs completed, both with respond artifacts

scenarios.reply_summary: Reply politely declines ('申し訳ありませんが、私は画像を生成する機能を持っていません。') — rubric content met. Delivered inline, no direct_llm skill.
scenarios.events_issues: Artifact direct_llm: NOT present. Router answered inline without spawning skill.
scenarios.artifact_check: FAIL — no direct_llm skill run; inline decline without skill dispatch

new_findings.title: Inline-reply path bypasses skill spawn for simple queries
new_findings.detail: S1, S5, S7 (and S3 via list_actions) all used chat_turn_completed_inline without spawning direct_llm. Rubric reply-content was met but artifact checks fail because no skill was dispatched. This is a structural pattern: short/simple prompts are answered inline by the router without routing_decided or skill_run_spawned events. The scenario rubric artifact checks assume direct_llm is always spawned, creating a systemic mismatch.
new_findings.hypothesis_confidence: HIGH — observed in 4/7 scenarios with consistent event pattern

new_findings.title: S5 (poem request) returns clarification question instead of poem
new_findings.detail: S5 asked for 'a short poem' (短い詩を書いてください). LLM asked for theme/mood clarification instead of writing one. This is a behavioral pattern — flash-lite model asks for disambiguation rather than executing the creative task directly.

new_findings.title: S3 skill discovery uses list_actions not routing_decided
new_findings.detail: When asked to list available skills, the router called list_actions tool directly and returned inline, bypassing the routing_decided event path. The scenario expects routing_decided but the actual OS behavior routes skill-listing queries to the list_actions action tool, not via skill dispatch.
